title: Automation to notify task owner of timeline change
excerpt: I am wanting to set up an automation that notifies the task owner if the timeline changes. However, I can’t see that this is an option: I can see how to set an automation that allows me to choose who is notified, but not…

posts.author: CJD (Cath Davies)
posts.cookedHtml: I am wanting to set up an automation that notifies the task owner if the timeline changes. However, I can’t see that this is an option: I can see how to set an automation that allows me to choose who is notified, but not the task owner, which will vary from task to task. Is this possible, please? Thanks, Cath

posts.author: malia.monday (Malia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Cath! To confirm, is the task owner assigned to the item through a People Column?

If so, you can select this in the automation recipe to notify the assignee:

Screenshot 2024-09-16 at 2.32.35 PM889×535 23.3 KB

posts.author: kaylak (Kayla K)
posts.cookedHtml:
@CJD ,

By task owner do you mean the person who created/entered the item?

posts.author: CJD (Cath Davies)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks Malia. Yes, that’s sorted it, thank you!

posts.author: CJD (Cath Davies)
posts.cookedHtml: Brilliant, thank you! I hadn’t previously clicked on “people” in order to then choose “owner”…

posts.author: abuislam (abuislam)
posts.cookedHtml: I don’t think the platform currently supports notifying the task owner directly through standard automations. You might want to check the documentation or contact support to explore custom solutions or upcoming features.

posts.author: malia.monday (Malia)
posts.cookedHtml: You’re welcome! Glad to hear this worked for what you needed
